---
title: "Week 1"
author: "Fred Azizi"
date: "2022-08-31"
output: 
   beamer_presentation:
     theme: "Frankfurt"
     colortheme: "crane"
header-includes:
- \usepackage{xcolor}
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)
```

## Who am I?

My name: Farideddin Azizi (You can call me Fred)

My pronouns: He/him/his

I am a PhD student in the Department of Statistics.

## What are we going to do here?

*   Structure of the class

   + Q/A- Working on worksheet in class- Working on additional worksheet in your time.

* Roughly, this is how the class time will be divided into:

   + \~10 to 20 min: Q/A about the material. 

   + \~ 30 to 40 min: Work on the new worksheet.

## Questions

**Questions:**

1- How is this class going to affect my grade?

2- Is attendance required?

3- How do I get the slides/worksheets?

4- Will you provide the solutions?

## Where do I get slides/worksheets/solution

Please scan this QR code:

![](frame.png){width="109"}

Or simply visit:

[fredazizi.github.io](fredazizi.github.io)

## Chapter 1: Quick Review (1)

## Chapter 1: Quick Review (2)

## Chapter 1: Quick Review (3)

## Question 1

## Question 2

## Worksheet

Lets take a look at the in-class worksheet. 

## Chapter 1: **key Concepts**

-   \textcolor{red}{Descriptive Statistics}: Descriptive statistics deals with methods of \textcolor{red}{organizing, summarizing, and presenting data} in a convenient and informative way.
    -   You can use numerical techniques to summarize data (mean, median, variance, etc)
    -   You can use graphical techniques to present data (Bar chart, scatterplot, words cloud, etc)
-   \textcolor{red}{Inferencial Statistics}: Inferential statistics is a body of methods used to \textcolor{red}{draw conclusions or inferences about characteristics of populations based on sample data.}
-   A \textcolor{red}{population} is the group of all items of interest to a statistics practitioner.
-   A descriptive measure of a population is called a \textcolor{red}{parameter}.
-   A \textcolor{red}{sample} is a set of data drawn from the studied population.
-   A descriptive measure of a sample is called a \textcolor{red}{statistic}.
